economic_notes: First Nation operates three commercial businesses: Muscowpetung Stop & Go Store (convenience store), Mino Maskihki Cannabis Dispensary, and Muscowpetung Security Services. Has 8.3% interest in Keseechiwan Holdings LP and 4.41% interest in Kief Cannabis Company Ltd. Has initiated a surrender claim and channelization claim against the Government of Canada with loans to fund settlement negotiations.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED - auditors unable to obtain sufficient evidence to verify completeness of revenues, expenses, assets and liabilities for the three commercial businesses (Stop & Go Store, Cannabis Dispensary) which have an August 31 year-end and were consolidated using August 31, 2020 financial information, excluding Sept 1 2020 to March 31 2021 operations. Comparative figures restated per Note 18. Two large loans outstanding for land claims (surrender claim and channelization claim) against Government of Canada totalling approximately $3.67M. Current liabilities include bank indebtedness $188,864, operating loan $299,984, accounts payable $1,380,792, deferred revenue ISC $2,335,602, deferred revenue other $829,212, current LTD $319,806, current CMHC $164,992. Annual debt service calculated as LTD repayment $141,370 plus CMHC repayment $163,120 = $304,490. Total government transfers include ISC $8,939,254 + CMHC $430,563 + FHQ Tribal Council $619,782 + Yorkton Tribal Council CFS $77,175 + QBOW CFS $55,907 + Living Sky CDC $32331 + GoC $0 = $10,155,012 plus other potential government sources; osr approximated accordingly. The Muscowpetung Legacy Trust draws repayable of $1,917,334 is an unusual item - treated as revenue but repayable.
